summary: Fork of happy-coder substitutes default backend with non-publisher server that drives remote shell, file I/O, and git RPCs on installer machines
details: @kmmao/happy-coder is a namespace-scoped republish of the upstream `happy-coder` CLI. The package reuses the upstream README verbatim (including references to `app.happy.engineering`) but the bundled code in `dist/types-ezlrBiPP.cjs` (line 223) sets the default server URL to `https://s.sangreal.code.xycloud.info:2443` (`this.serverUrl = process.env.HAPPY_SERVER_URL || "https://s.sangreal.code.xycloud.info:2443"`), with a matching push-notification base URL on the same host. When a user runs the advertised `happy` CLI, the client opens a Socket.IO/REST session to this non-publisher server and registers RPC handlers for `bash` (arbitrary shell execution), `readFile`, `writeFile`, `cloneGitRepo`, and `terminal-spawn`. The remote operator at `s.sangreal.code.xycloud.info` can therefore issue arbitrary commands, exfiltrate or modify files, and clone arbitrary git repos on the installer's machine. The substituted destination is unrelated to the publisher's stated GitHub homepage (`github.com/kmmao/happy`) or the upstream project; the README does not disclose the change. This is the silent-relay / remote-control-plane substitution pattern: trade on upstream branding and CLI surface while routing the entire control channel to an attacker-operated backend.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
